macro_segments.title: Introduction to the Mishnah on Shevuat Bituy and the Scope of Oaths

micro_segments.title: Opening and Daf Overview: The Parameters of Shevuat Bituy
Mishnah: Four Categories of Shevuat Bituy (Self vs. Others; Substance vs. Non-substance)
Examples from the Mishnah: Giving Money, Sleeping, Throwing a Rock into the Sea
Debate: Rabbi Yishmael (Future Only) vs. Rabbi Akiva (Past and Future)

macro_segments.title: Comparison of Vows (Nedarim) and Oaths (Shevuot) — Respective Stringencies

micro_segments.title: Stringencies of Vows (Nedarim) Over Oaths: Effect on Mitzvah Objects
Stringencies of Oaths (Shevuot) Over Vows: Applicability to Non-Substance

macro_segments.title: Examination of the Mishnah's Examples in the Gemara: Giving, Sleeping, Throwing a Rock

micro_segments.title: Giving to a Poor Person: Why It Must Be a Gift to a Rich Person (Obligatory Charity Exception)
Sleeping: Shevuat Shav vs. Shevuat Bituy (Three Days vs. Unqualified Oath)
Throwing a Rock into the Sea (Past Tense): Debate Between Rav and Shmuel on Past Oaths

macro_segments.title: The Debate of Rav and Shmuel: Must a Past Oath Be Formulable in the Future?

micro_segments.title: Rav's Position: An Oath in the Past (Ploni Threw a Rock) Is Valid If Reversible (Lav v'Hein)
Shmuel's Position: A Past Oath Requires Formulability in the Future (Lehaba Criterion)
Gemara Clarification: Debate Is Within Rabbi Akiva's Framework, Not Between Yishmael and Akiva
Shmuel's Reasoning: The Paradigm of Lehara U'Leheitiv Requires Future Orientation

macro_segments.title: Rav Hamnuna's Challenge to Shmuel: The Case of 'I Did Not Put on Tefillin Today'

micro_segments.title: Proof from Mishnah: 'I Did Not Eat Today and I Did Not Put on Tefillin Today'
Problem for Shmuel: 'I Will Not Put on Tefillin' Cannot Be Formulable (Lav Lehaba)
Shmuel's Answer: Tefillin Case Is Only for Malkus, Not for Korban Oleh V'Yored

macro_segments.title: Rava's Inquiry: Shevuat Shav vs. Shevuat Bituy (Stone Painted as Gold Example)

micro_segments.title: Definition of Shevuat Shav: Oath on Patently False or True Matter (Three People Know)
Question: If Stone Is Not Obviously Stone (Painted), Does It Count as Shevuat Bituy?
Rava's Answer: If Obvious, It Is Shevuat Shav (Malkus); If Not, It Is Shevuat Sheker, Not Bituy

macro_segments.title: Abaye on the Relationship Between Lav V'Hein and Shevuat Bituy: The Testimony Case

micro_segments.title: Case: 'I Know Testimony for You' — Why Is It Exempt (Patur) According to Rav?
Abaye's Innovation: 'I Know' and 'I Don't Know' Are Different Categories (Not Lav V'Hein)
Past Oaths in Lav V'Hein Without Future: Yadati v'Lo Yadati vs. He'adati v'Lo He'adati

macro_segments.title: Why Did the Torah Create a Separate Category of Shevuat Ha'edut?

micro_segments.title: Shmuel's Explanation: Shevuat Ha'edut Exists Because Past Testimony Cannot Be Made Future-Oriented
Rav's Problem: Why Create a Separate Category If Past Oaths Are Valid (No Lehaba Requirement)?
Abaye's Answer: Shevuat Ha'edut Was Created to Allow Chiyuv Even for Mezid (Willful Violation)
The Criteria of 'Ne'elam' (Hidden): Different for Shevuat Ha'edut (No Ne'elam Requirement)

macro_segments.title: The Apparent Contradiction in Abaye: Is Shevuat Ha'edut Also Shevuat Bituy?

micro_segments.title: Earlier Statement by Abaye: 'I Know Testimony' Is Exempt Because Negative Form Is Shevuat Ha'edut, Not Bituy
Later Implication: Shevuat Ha'edut Is Its Own Category (Not Lav V'Hein), So Only One Korban
The Contradiction Resolved: Abaye Retracted; Shevuat Ha'edut Is Both Ha'edut and Bituy
Alternative: One Statement from Rav Pappa, One from Abaye; Or One View That Only Ha'edut Applies
